subject: Set certificate SAN as msb-iag
commit_message:
Set certificate SAN as msb-iag

Also add helm certificate to be used within deployments.

Issue-ID: MSB-467
Signed-off-by: Huabing Zhao <zhao.huabing@zte.com.cn>
Signed-off-by: Konrad Bańka <k.banka@samsung.com>
Change-Id: I1dabc31275edebd4a01be658e462a7d45497ce46

entries.message: Uploaded patch set 1.

Patch Set 1:

Gitlab controller: Deployment put on queue, number 3 on the queue.

Patch Set 1:

Build Started https://jenkins.onap.org/job/oom-master-verify-helm/4895/

Patch Set 1:

Hi Konrad, 

The certificates and private key paths in the msb-iag and msb-eag docker are the following：

Server certificate and key：
/usr/local/openresty/nginx/ssl/cert/cert.crt
/usr/local/openresty/nginx/ssl/cert/cert.key

Root CA：
/usr/local/openresty/nginx/html/cert/ca.crt

Patch Set 1: Verified+1

Build Successful 

https://jenkins.onap.org/job/oom-master-verify-helm/4895/ : SUCCESS

Logs: https://logs.onap.org/production/vex-yul-ecomp-jenkins-1/oom-master-verify-helm/4895

Uploaded patch set 2.

Patch Set 2:

Gitlab controller: Deployment put on queue, number 3 on the queue.

Patch Set 2: Code-Review-1

Hi Huabing, thanks for the patch.

I've updated deployments to use certs provided in oom but I have some issues with verifying this locally, so I'm putting `-1` until I verify it works properly.

As I understand the certificate key doesn't need to be updated (compared to the one in image) as it was not provided here in the repo?

Patch Set 2:

Build Started https://jenkins.onap.org/job/oom-master-verify-helm/4911/

Patch Set 2:

> Patch Set 2: Code-Review-1
> 
> Hi Huabing, thanks for the patch.
> 
> I've updated deployments to use certs provided in oom but I have some issues with verifying this locally, so I'm putting `-1` until I verify it works properly.
> 
> As I understand the certificate key doesn't need to be updated (compared to the one in image) as it was not provided here in the repo?

Yes, that's correct. The private key didn't change. You can verify the new certificate with the old key.

Healthchecks failed: VFC; K8s jobs failed - Netbox provisioning. Some smoke usecases also failed...
@Sylvain/@Krzysztof - is it a fine criteria to pass Gating CI now or is redeploy needed?

Patch Set 3: Code-Review+2

> Patch Set 3:
> 
> > Patch Set 3:
> > 
> > Gitlab Orange 2: 
> > Full results can be seen here: https://gating-results.onap.eu/results//107649-3/index.html
> 
> Healthchecks failed: VFC; K8s jobs failed - Netbox provisioning. Some smoke usecases also failed...
> @Sylvain/@Krzysztof - is it a fine criteria to pass Gating CI now or is redeploy needed?

Yeah it looks fine. netbox is failing but I know the reason. I've cooked a patch to fix that:
https://gerrit.onap.org/r/c/oom/+/107923

Patch Set 3: Code-Review-1

Ok I see that SO is not the best one in terms of properly failing on wrong configuration. I expect that this patch introduces some regression in SO so could you please squash it with the one where you change so config? And if there are any other components impacted by this change they should be also included

Patch Set 3:

> Patch Set 3: Code-Review-1
> 
> Ok I see that SO is not the best one in terms of properly failing on wrong configuration. I expect that this patch introduces some regression in SO so could you please squash it with the one where you change so config? And if there are any other components impacted by this change they should be also included

Hi Krzysztof,

Do you mean I should squash it with this review - https://gerrit.onap.org/r/c/oom/+/106826/3? Basically it's fine for me but I thought it would be better to separate two related, but different changes in two commits. Let me know and I'll update reviews then 😊

Patch Set 3: Code-Review+1

+1 for functionality.
I have tested this along with related patch https://gerrit.onap.org/r/c/oom/+/106826/3

Testing notes were added to https://jira.onap.org/browse/SO-2922

Patch Set 3: Code-Review+1

Just the vfc HC failure that we observe from time to time but let's rebase to be 100% sure
